description_long: This is a pair of heavy-duty slip-joint pliers, featuring long, straight handles with a worn red coating and visible signs of use. The handles are robust and slightly flattened, providing a solid grip, while the metal surface shows patches of rust and wear, indicating extensive use in urban or industrial environments. The jaws are broad and serrated, designed for gripping and turning objects of various sizes, with a slip-joint mechanism that allows for adjustable jaw width. The pivot point is prominent and reinforced, ensuring durability and strength during operation. The overall construction is metallic, likely steel, with a utilitarian design focused on function over aesthetics. No visible branding or logo is present on the tool.
description: A worn, heavy-duty pair of slip-joint pliers with long red handles and serrated jaws, showing signs of extensive use and rust.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open jaws and the broad, serrated gripping surfaces, with both handles visible and angled outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the straight handles and the slip-joint pivot, with the jaws partially visible.
description_view_2: Angled view focusing on the serrated jaws and the robust pivot mechanism, with the handles receding into the background.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the length and wear of the handles, as well as the alignment of the jaws and pivot.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight, flattened handles with a worn red painted coating; not insulated or ergonomic.
attribute_jaw_design: Broad, serrated jaws with a slip-joint adjustment; designed for gripping and turning, not needle-nose or diagonal.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Slip-joint pivot mechanism allowing for adjustable jaw width; robust and reinforced.
attribute_grip_pattern: Handles have a smooth, painted surface with visible wear; no additional texturing or rubberized grip.
